schema_version: primary_cause, moon_role, mechanism, sun_role, spring_neap_dependence, earth_motion_required, quantitative

primary_cause.quote: The tides...are caused by the sun and the moon

moon_role.quote: Never are the waters moved but the moon draws them

mechanism.quote: As the moon rises the seas swell, and again they sink as she declines from the meridian

sun_role.quote: The sun's influence is joined to hers, adding to the swelling at the conjunctions

spring_neap_dependence.quote: The tides are greatest at the new and the full moon

primary_cause.quote: The moon it is that lifts the waters and lets them fall

moon_role.quote: The moon it is that lifts the waters and lets them fall

mechanism.quote: The ocean is the companion of the moon's course: with her rising it comes on, with her setting it withdraws

spring_neap_dependence.quote: At the new moon and the full the springs run highest

primary_cause.quote: The moon, being cognate to the waters of the earth, attracts them

moon_role.quote: The moon, being cognate to the waters of the earth, attracts them

mechanism.quote: The tides, therefore, are the attraction of the waters by the moon

primary_cause.quote: The tides follow from the Earth's motion

moon_role.quote: The moon having nothing to do with it

mechanism.quote: The continual alteration of speed makes the contained waters run now toward one end of their basins, now toward the other

earth_motion_required.quote: The tides follow from the Earth's motion, and that a motionless Earth could show no tides at all

primary_cause.quote: The sun, straitening the vortex likewise, augments the effect at the new and full moon

moon_role.quote: By the pressure of the subtle matter that the tides are caused

mechanism.quote: The fluid heaven presses more strongly upon the waters lying under the moon, and depresses them there

sun_role.quote: The sun, straitening the vortex likewise, augments the effect at the new and full moon

spring_neap_dependence.quote: The sun augments the effect at the new and full moon

primary_cause.quote: The flux and reflux of the sea arise from the actions of the sun and moon

moon_role.quote: The sun and moon, by their gravitation, draw up the waters of the sea

mechanism.quote: The sun and moon, by their gravitation, draw up the waters of the sea

sun_role.quote: The moon's force to raise the sea is greater than the sun's, being nearer the earth

spring_neap_dependence.quote: When the sun and moon are in conjunction or opposition, their forces conspire, and the greatest, or spring, tides are produced

quantitative.quote: The heights of the tides may be computed from the masses and distances of the two luminaries

primary_cause.quote: The tides are the oscillations of the sea produced by the gravitational attractions of the sun and the moon

moon_role.quote: The gravitational attractions of the sun and the moon

mechanism.quote: The waters respond to the periodic forces of the two bodies as a dynamical system of forced oscillations

quantitative.quote: One must integrate the differential equations of the moving ocean; from it the observed heights and times at each port may be deduced by calculation
